title: Dynamic Tools — ToolLoader, plugin directories, hot-reload, runtime tool management

Semantic Search — pure embedding-based search with metadata filtering
Advanced RAG — PDFs, SQLite persistent storage, custom chunking, metadata filtering
Multi-Provider RAG Comparison — compare embeddings, stores, and chunk sizes across providers

desc: Demonstrates: 1. FallbackProvider with priority ordering

title: Batch Processing — Classify multiple requests concurrently

title: Guardrails Engine
desc: Demonstrates input and output guardrails for content validation, PII redaction, topic blocking, and format enforcement.

title: Audit Logging
desc: Demonstrates JSONL audit logging with privacy controls and daily file rotation.

title: Tool Output Screening
desc: Demonstrates prompt injection detection in tool outputs, both per-tool and global screening.

title: Coherence Checking
desc: Demonstrates LLM-based intent verification that catches tool calls diverging from the user's original request (prompt injection defence).

title: Persistent Sessions — Save and restore conversation memory across agent instances
desc: Demonstrates JsonFileSessionStore: the agent's conversation history is persisted to disk and restored when a new agent is created with the same session_id.

title: Summarize-on-Trim — Automatically summarize old messages when memory is trimmed
desc: When the conversation exceeds max_messages, the oldest messages are removed. With summarize_on_trim=True the agent asks an LLM to condense them into a

title: Entity Memory — Extract and track named entities across conversation turns
desc: EntityMemory merges entities into a deduplicated registry and builds a context block for the system prompt. This example manually feeds entities to demonstrate

title: Knowledge Graph Memory — Track relationship triples across conversation turns
desc: KnowledgeGraphMemory stores subject-relation-object triples in a TripleStore. Relevant triples are queried each turn and injected into the system prompt.

title: Knowledge Memory — Persistent cross-session facts with daily logs
desc: KnowledgeMemory stores daily log entries and persistent facts in MEMORY.md. When configured on an agent, a ``remember`` tool is auto-registered and the

desc: Demonstrates how to stop the agent loop after a specific tool fires, without making another LLM call. Two mechanisms:

desc: ==================================== Evaluate your agent's accuracy, tool use, latency, cost, and safety

desc: ======================================================== Turn any selectools @tool function into an MCP-compliant server.

title: Token Budget Per Run — stop agents before they burn money
desc: Demonstrates: - max_total_tokens: hard limit on cumulative tokens

title: Agent Cancellation — cooperative stopping from any thread
desc: Demonstrates: - CancellationToken for cooperative cancellation

title: Per-Tool Approval Gate — require human approval for dangerous tools
desc: Demonstrates: - @tool(requires_approval=True) decorator flag

title: SimpleStepObserver — single callback for all agent events
desc: Demonstrates: - SimpleStepObserver routes 31 events to one function

title: Token Estimation — estimate costs before running an agent
desc: Demonstrates: - estimate_tokens() for single strings

title: Model Switching — use different models per iteration
desc: Demonstrates: - model_selector callback on AgentConfig

title: Knowledge Memory Stores — persistent knowledge with importance and TTL
desc: Demonstrates: - KnowledgeMemory with SQLiteKnowledgeStore backend

title: Reasoning Strategies — ReAct, Chain-of-Thought, and Plan-Then-Act
desc: Demonstrates: - reasoning_strategy="react"  — Thought → Action → Observation cycle

title: Tool Result Caching — avoid re-executing expensive tools

desc: Demonstrates: - SemanticCache as a drop-in replacement for InMemoryCache

title: Prompt Compression — prevent context-window overflow in long conversations
desc: Demonstrates: - compress_context=True — enable proactive context compression

title: Conversation Branching — fork conversation history for A/B exploration
desc: Demonstrates: - ConversationMemory.branch() — snapshot memory for safe experimentation

title: Linear AgentGraph pipeline
desc: Demonstrates a simple 3-node linear graph: planner → writer → reviewer → END

title: Parallel fan-out with AgentGraph
desc: Demonstrates parallel execution of multiple agents with state merging: entry → [researcher_a, researcher_b, researcher_c] → summarizer → END

title: Conditional routing with AgentGraph
desc: Demonstrates conditional edges with path_map validation: drafter → router → (revise | publish) → END

title: Human-in-the-loop with AgentGraph
desc: Demonstrates generator nodes with yield InterruptRequest: - Graph pauses at reviewer node

title: Checkpointing with AgentGraph
desc: Demonstrates durable mid-graph persistence using FileCheckpointStore: - Save checkpoints after each node

title: SupervisorAgent with multiple coordination strategies
desc: Demonstrates all four supervisor strategies: - plan_and_execute: LLM generates a plan, then executes each step

title: Nested subgraphs with AgentGraph
desc: Demonstrates SubgraphNode — an AgentGraph embedded as a node in another graph. Uses input_map and output_map for explicit state key translation.

title: Loading an Agent from YAML config
desc: Demonstrates the structured AgentConfig workflow (v0.19.0): - Write a YAML config file describing the agent

title: Agent Templates
desc: Demonstrates the template system (v0.19.0): - List available built-in templates

title: Serving an Agent over HTTP
desc: Demonstrates the `selectools serve` pattern (v0.19.0): - Create an agent with tools

title: Tool Composition with compose()
desc: Demonstrates composing multiple tools into a single composite tool (v0.19.0): - Define individual tools with @tool()

title: Streaming Pipeline with astream()
desc: Demonstrates pipeline streaming (v0.19.0): - Build a multi-step pipeline with @step

title: Type-Safe Pipeline Contracts
desc: Demonstrates type-safe step contracts in pipelines (v0.19.0): - Steps infer input/output types from type hints

title: PostgresCheckpointStore for AgentGraph
desc: Demonstrates using PostgreSQL as a checkpoint backend (v0.19.0): - Create a PostgresCheckpointStore with a connection string

title: Trace Storage and Querying
desc: Demonstrates saving and querying agent traces (v0.19.0): - InMemoryTraceStore for development

title: PlanAndExecuteAgent
desc: The planner Agent generates a JSON execution plan. Executor agents handle each step in sequence. Results are aggregated into a final output.

desc: Multiple agents argue opposing positions over max_rounds rounds. Each agent sees the prior round transcript so they can respond to each other. A judge

title: TeamLeadAgent
desc: The lead Agent generates a subtask plan and delegates to team members. Three delegation strategies are demonstrated:

title: trace_to_html — HTML Trace Viewer
desc: Renders an AgentTrace as a standalone HTML waterfall timeline. No external dependencies — the output is a single self-contained HTML file.

title: Stability Markers — @stable, @beta, @deprecated
desc: The stability module provides three decorators for annotating the public API stability of any class or function.
